objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 SIS webhook assessment where unsigned roster events can update guardian contact records.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real external target is contacted.
Students, guardian contacts, webhook events, and secrets are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map SIS webhook ingestion controls and documented signature policy.
actions: Review the synthetic webhook policy endpoint
Identify whether event signatures are enforced or only logged
Record baseline guardian-contact state before replay
successCriteria: Webhook policy and baseline guardian evidence are recorded
noSubagents: school-impact interpretation stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate an unsigned SIS event can modify a guardian contact.
actions: Replay a synthetic guardian-contact update without an HMAC signature
Confirm the event is accepted through the trusted-source bypass
Fetch the student guardian record to prove the unauthorized state change
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites policy, unsigned webhook acceptance, and resulting guardian record evidence
noSubagents: final exploitability and remediation wording stay with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic SIS webhook policy response
evidence.summary: The webhook policy states signatures are logged as warnings instead of enforced when the trusted-source bypass is present.

evidence.title: Synthetic unsigned SIS guardian update accepted
evidence.summary: An unsigned guardian-contact update was accepted when the trusted-source bypass parameter was supplied.

evidence.title: Synthetic guardian contact changed after webhook replay
evidence.summary: The student guardian record reflected the attacker-controlled email after the unsigned webhook replay.

findings.title: SIS webhook accepts unsigned guardian-contact updates
findings.description: The synthetic SIS webhook endpoint does not require a valid HMAC signature when a trusted-source bypass parameter is present, allowing unsigned events to update guardian contact records.
findings.impact: An attacker who can reach the webhook endpoint could replace guardian contact details, intercept family communications, or create a path toward unauthorized guardian-account linking.
findings.remediation: Require HMAC verification for every SIS webhook event, remove source-based bypasses, reject unsigned or invalidly signed events before state changes, constrain event schemas, and alert on repeated signature failures.
